sources.ybl: Yale Budget Lab · 'AI Exposure: What do we know?' (Feb 2026) PCA-weighted score
sources.robotics: Heuristic by SOC major group; Webb pct_robots not currently accessible
sources.bls: BLS Employment Projections 2024-34 (Aug 2025) + OEWS National May 2024 — hand-curated subset

featured_pairs.label: Paralegal vs. Nurse Practitioner
featured_pairs.blurb: Same credential tier, opposite outcomes. The clearest illustration that 'more education = safer' no longer holds.

featured_pairs.label: Computer Programmer vs. Software Developer
featured_pairs.blurb: Same SOC major group. AI redraws within a discipline — automating the codable part, leaving design and architecture.

featured_pairs.label: Cashier vs. Fast Food Worker
featured_pairs.blurb: Both ~$31k, no credential. Shows how the speed of robotics deployment differs even between adjacent service jobs.

featured_pairs.label: Customer Service Rep vs. Home Health Aide
featured_pairs.blurb: Two of the largest occupations in the US economy heading in opposite directions.

featured_pairs.label: Truck Driver vs. Electrician
featured_pairs.blurb: Both blue-collar, ~$60k, HS-level. Physical AI is a different story than embodied factory robotics.

featured_pairs.label: Graphic Designer vs. Cosmetologist
featured_pairs.blurb: Lower-credentialed work proves more secure than higher-credentialed creative work — the inversion in one comparison.

featured_pairs.label: Warehouse Stocker vs. Welder
featured_pairs.blurb: Same blue-collar tier, different exposures. High robotics exposure ≠ near-term displacement when demand growth is fast.

occupations.title: Computer Programmers
occupations.narrative: Generative AI coding tools take on routine programming tasks. BLS revised projection from positive growth to -10% as junior coding work gets automated.

occupations.title: Software Developers
occupations.narrative: Demand for full-stack development, system architecture, and AI integration grows even as code generation gets cheaper — productivity gains expected to drive new project starts.

occupations.title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
Customer Service Representatives
occupations.narrative: AI assistants and chatbots automate Tier-1 ticket triage. Klarna's 700-agent equivalent in 2024 was a bellwether — partial reversal in 2025 shows the limits.

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
Paralegals and Legal Assistants
occupations.narrative: BLS notes AI tools (large language models) increasingly handle document review, contract analysis, and case research, suppressing demand growth that previously trended +14%.

occupations.title: Graphic Designers
occupations.narrative: Generative image models compress production timelines on routine assets. Higher-end design and brand work increasingly differentiated.

occupations.title: Secondary School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
Educational, Guidance, and Career Counselors and Advisors
Nurse Practitioners
occupations.narrative: Aging population drives sustained demand. NPs are licensed to provide many physician services at lower cost. Tasks remain high-touch, low-AI-exposure.

occupations.title: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers
Elementary School Teachers, Except Special Education
First-Line Supervisors of Production and Operating Workers
First-Line Supervisors of Construction Trades and Extraction Workers
First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
Licensed Practical and Licensed Vocational Nurses
Heavy and Tractor-Trailer Truck Drivers
occupations.narrative: BLS notes long-haul trucking faces eventual exposure to autonomous driving, but commercial deployment timelines remain uncertain. Drayage and final-mile remain robust.

occupations.title: Stockers and Order Fillers
occupations.narrative: Amazon now operates >1 million robots in fulfillment (July 2025). Despite high robotics exposure, e-commerce demand growth keeps absolute headcount rising near-term.

occupations.title: Electricians
occupations.narrative: Skilled trade with strong physical-task component. Aging workforce + sustained construction demand keep growth positive.

occupations.title: Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists
occupations.narrative: In-person service. Low automation exposure. Steady growth from population and discretionary spending.
